name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist specializing in polymer chemistry with 6+ years of experience in polymer synthesis, characterization, and materials analysis. Expertise in FT-IR, thermal analysis (TGA, DSC), GPC, and spectroscopic techniques for polymer systems. Strong background in structure-property relationships, polymer thin-films, and surface functionalization. Experienced in process development for polymer materials and statistical optimization methods (DOE, SPC).

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

bullets: Lead process development for bio-based polymer systems using FT-IR, GPC, and thermal analysis for characterization.

experience.company: University of Colorado Boulder / Georgia Tech
bullets: Characterized polymer systems using FT-IR, TGA, DSC, GPC, and ellipsometry to establish structure-property relationships.
Developed CVD-based polymer thin-film deposition processes with controlled morphology and properties.

skills.Polymer Characterization: FT-IR, TGA, DSC, GPC, ellipsometry, QCM-D, NMR
skills.Polymer Chemistry: Polymer synthesis, thin films, surface functionalization, structure-property relationships

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Co-led process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.
